reviews.review_msg: Great class and professor in general. Very interesting subjects. He basically tells you stories the whole class time. You may find yourself taking a lot of notes down if you don't have a good memory. Readings are not mandatory. A fair to easy marker on essays. Very helpful and informative. I would definitely take another course with him.
Super nice, good marker, very interested and knowledgeable about subject. Doesn't always give super clear instructions but very willing to go over things you don't understand.

Everything he says in lecture you've already read in the textbook. Horrible essay questions, far too hard and expects too much from a 1st year course. That said, it's a relatively easy science credit and he is entertaining enough to keep you awake in lecture. I'd take it again, but mainly because my TA was awesome!
One word: AWFUL! Assignments are confusing and he marks harder than any prof I've had (I'm in 3rd year). Wouldn't recommend this course.

He covers the entire technological history of the world - 2.7 million years ago to now.  Much to broad for my liking and made it very hard to study for the final because of the vastness of the material.  But he was an easy marker and there was not alot of work for this class making the class ok overall.

This class was quite challenging for someone like me who is scientifically inept. Textbook is informative, but would be better if it covered key words and concepts throughout rather than reading like a novel. Essays were fairly marked, but the final was absolutely killer. Hardest test I've ever written, even though I studied a lot.

He's not a bad prof, seems to know what he's talking about. Course (Hist of science) is pretty interesting and the course load is average, 3 4 page papers that get harder as term goes on. Would only recommend the course if you have an interest in the subject, otherwise you`ll find it dull.

Terrible proff! Most of the material he teaches is very vague, questions for the assignments are not stated clearly. He wrote the textbook and will ask to write papers based on his textbook. What will help is taking the same position as him, cause he marks papers. He is not interested in subject and is sluggish. I would not recommened taking the cl

Bad! Bad! Bad! Bad! Bad!  Arrogant, pompous (he wears wizard robes to class), and honestly he doesn't seem that bright.  He wrote the course textbook...seemingly in less time than this review is taking me to write.  That said, the class is fantastic despite him.

Great prof. He's to the point and if you follow his criteria you will do well. Tutorial isn't mandatory which is a plus! If you do the readings and have a logical flow when you write your papers, you will succeed.

A great professor. Lectures focus on interesting aspects of material. Pretty easy marker but a little disorganized. Text (he co-authored) is well written and easy to read. No midterm, three 4 page papers. Highly recommended prof/class.

Very clear and intersting lectures. Really helpful with any questions regarding assignments. It is easy to get good marks on his papers if you follow his instructions. Plus this course now counts as a B-SCI credit.
the easiest prof in History. He is very knowledgable! You will regret if you don't take his class in SFU!

He's awesome...totally worth taking one of his classes. He is fair helpful and very easy to understand. His seminar on technology was really interesting! You wouldn't think the subject could be fun but it is!
